calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-02-2023 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement [Cal. Code Civ. Proc. Section 664.6]
Off Calendar Oct-30-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert Matz, Esq.(510-263-8775) of Matz Law Group for Plaintiff.
Robert Charles Ward, Esq. (415-421-6500) of Shartsis Friese LLP for Defendant.
S. James Lee, Esq. (415-632-1333) of Business Counsel Law Group specially appearing as general counsel for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues trademark issues on Burma Superstar, B star and Eats. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT DESMOND TAN's Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement [Cal. Code Civ. Proc. Section 664.6].

DEFENDANT DESMOND TAN'S Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement [per California Code of Civil Procedure Section 664.6] was granted in large part as reflected on the record at the prior hearings on September 8 and October 6. On the only remaining issues, The Court grants the motion in substantial part with modifications as discussed in the Order. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript) Order is signed.(see scanned order for details)Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, email:rhunter@sftc.org, Reported. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement [Cal. Code Civ. Proc. Section 664.6]
Off Calendar Oct-06-2017 Continued To Oct-30-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Robert Matz of the law offices of Matz Law Group (510-263-8775) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Robert Charles Ward of the law offices of Shartsis Friese LLP (415-421-6500) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

This motion to enforce settlement agreement was previously GRANTED in large part on September 8, 2017. Counsel were previously directed to meet and confer regarding modifications to the proposed order and/or judgment as discussed on Sep-8-2017. The Court continued the matter to this date for further hearing and to allow parties to finalize a global settlement.

On this date, the Court inquires and counsel report that they failed to reach an agreement. Court and counsel discuss defendant's request for correction of proposed judgment as to specific trademark entity. The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. Defendant submits two alternate proposed orders in open court. Court indicates it intends to sign the proposed order submitted by Mr. Ward with modifications on October 30, 2017, but allows counsel to brief the matter further.

DEFENDANT DESMOND TAN'S Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement [Cal. Code Civ. Proc. Section 664.6] is CONTINUED to October 30, 2017 at 9:30 AM in Departmenet 302. Defendant may file a supplemental brief no later than October 12. Plaintiff may file opposition no later than October 20, Defendant may reply no later than October 25.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Gena Eales, CSR No. 9176, e-mail: geales@sftc.org. (L&M302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement [Cal. Code Civ. Proc. Section 664.6]
Off Calendar Sep-15-2017 Continued To Oct-06-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT DESMOND TAN'S Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement [Cal. Code Civ. Proc. Section 664.6] is Continued to October 6, 2017 at 9:30 AM in Department 302.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Not reported. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement [Cal. Code Civ. Proc. Section 664.6]
Off Calendar Sep-08-2017 Continued To Sep-15-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert Charles Ward, Esq. of Shartsis Friese LLP, Tel. 415.421.6500, for defendant and moving party;
Robert Matz, Esq. of the Matz Law Group, Tel. 510.263.8775, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted in full its tentative ruling, while deferring it until the next hearing, as follows: DEFENDANT DESMOND TAN's Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement is granted in substantial part. Judgment will be entered in accordance with the terms set forth in the settlement agreement dated March 24, 2017 with the modifications discussed in this order. Both parties agree that the March 24, 2017 settlement agreement is binding and should be entered into a judgment. Due to the parties' inability to agree on the formal paperwork memorializing their settlement and Mr. Tan's offer to make the first payment into escrow pending resolution of the parties' disputes, it would be unfair to permit Ms. Lee to invoke the payment acceleration language in the settlement agreement due to the first payment not having been made to her prior to this date. The judgment will provide that the first payment of $100,000 must be made by September 18, 2017 rather than June 15, 2017. The judgment will also provide that Mr. Tan is obligated to pay Mr. Matz $19,219.80 for his services in defending Ms. Lee in the class action case pending in Alameda County, Mr. Matx should immediately cease all work on that case, no further payment will be due to Mr. Matz for his work on that case, and no later than September 18, 2017 Mr. Tan or his counsel must provide a substitution of counsel for Ms. Lee in that case. Per CCP 664.6, the court interprets the irrevocable license granted to plaintiff Jocelyn Lee by the settlement agreement as not transferrable. The declarations of James Lee and Mr. Tan, which are admissible parol evidence to explain the terms of the settlement agreement, show that Mr. Tan rejected Ms. Lee's request for an assignable license. In these circumstances and given the absence of any language in the settlement agreement providing that Ms. Lee may assign the trademark, the rule that "a trademark licensee may not sub-license a mark without express permission from the licensor" governs the language of the parties' settlement. (Miller v. Glen Miller Productions, Inc. (9th Cir. 2006) 454 F.3d 945, 988.). No attorneys' fees are awarded to either side because neither side can fairly be characterized as the prevailing party on this motion."
In addition, the Court continued the matter to September 15, 2017 for further hearing and to allow parties to finalize a global settlement. If plaintiff's counsel is unavailable for hearing on September 15, the hearing shall be continued to October 6, 2017. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, rhunter@sftc.org.
